passages.title: The Science of Laughter
passages.text:
Laughter is a universal human behavior that transcends cultural boundaries. From the moment a baby first giggles at around three to four months of age, laughter becomes a fundamental part of human interaction. But what exactly is laughter, and why do we do it?

Contrary to popular belief, laughter is primarily a social behavior rather than a response to jokes or humor. Research has shown that people are about 30 times more likely to laugh when they are with others than when they are alone. This suggests that laughter serves an important social function, helping to establish and maintain bonds between individuals.

The physical act of laughter involves complex coordination of muscles, including the diaphragm, abdomen, and facial muscles. When we laugh, our breathing patterns change, often resulting in short, sharp exhalations. This process releases endorphins - the body's natural feel-good chemicals - which can reduce pain and promote feelings of wellbeing.

Neurological studies have identified specific regions of the brain involved in laughter processing. The prefrontal cortex helps with understanding context and social cues, while the limbic system processes the emotional components. Interestingly, the ability to detect genuine laughter versus fake laughter relies on different neural pathways.

Laughter also has documented health benefits. Studies indicate that regular laughter can boost the immune system, lower blood pressure, reduce stress hormones, and improve cardiovascular health. Some hospitals have incorporated laughter therapy into their treatment programs, recognizing its therapeutic potential.

Cross-cultural studies of laughter reveal both similarities and differences. While the physical expression of laughter is universal, what triggers laughter can vary significantly between cultures. However, certain types of humor - particularly those involving incongruity or surprise - seem to be nearly universal.

The evolutionary origin of laughter likely predates human language. Primates and even some rodents exhibit vocalizations that resemble laughter during rough-and-tumble play. This suggests that laughter has deep evolutionary roots as a signal of safety and playfulness, allowing individuals to distinguish between genuine threat and playful behavior.

questions.question: Laughter is primarily a response to jokes and humor.
questions.explanation: EN: The text states 'Contrary to popular belief, laughter is primarily a social behavior rather than a response to jokes or humor.'|CN: 文章指出'与普遍认知相反，笑主要是一种社交行为，而非对笑话或幽默的回应。'

questions.question: According to the passage, at what age do babies typically begin to laugh?
questions.explanation: EN: The text says a baby first giggles 'at around three to four months of age.'|CN: 文章说婴儿'大约在三四个月大的时候'第一次咯咯笑。

questions.question: People are approximately 30 times more likely to laugh when alone than with others.
questions.explanation: EN: The text says people are 30 times more likely to laugh when 'with others than when they are alone' - which is the opposite.|CN: 文章说人们在'与他人在一起时笑的概率是独处时的30倍'——这是相反的。

questions.question: Complete the sentence: Laughter releases _______, which are the body's natural feel-good chemicals.
questions.explanation: EN: The text states 'This process releases endorphins - the body's natural feel-good chemicals.'|CN: 文章指出'这个过程释放内啡肽——人体天然的快乐化学物质。'

questions.question: Which part of the brain helps with understanding context and social cues in laughter? (Two-word answer)
questions.explanation: EN: The text states 'The prefrontal cortex helps with understanding context and social cues.'|CN: 文章指出'前额叶皮层有助于理解语境和社交线索。'

questions.question: Laughter therapy is used in all hospitals worldwide.
questions.explanation: EN: The text says 'Some hospitals have incorporated laughter therapy' not all.|CN: 文章说'一些医院已将笑疗纳入治疗计划'，并非所有医院。

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT mentioned as a health benefit of laughter?
questions.explanation: EN: The text mentions immune system, blood pressure, and stress hormones, but not eyesight.|CN: 文章提到了免疫系统、血压和压力激素，但没有提到改善视力。

questions.question: What triggers laughter is the same across all cultures.
questions.explanation: EN: The text states 'what triggers laughter can vary significantly between cultures.'|CN: 文章指出'引发笑的因素在不同文化之间可能有显著差异。'

questions.question: Complete the sentence: The evolutionary origin of laughter likely predates human _______.
questions.explanation: EN: The text states 'The evolutionary origin of laughter likely predates human language.'|CN: 文章指出'笑的进化起源可能早于人类语言。'

questions.question: What does the passage suggest about the evolutionary function of laughter?
options: A. It developed to express joy
B. It serves as a signal of safety and playfulness
C. It evolved to help with language development
D. It was originally a response to physical tickling
questions.correctAnswer: B. It serves as a signal of safety and playfulness
questions.explanation: EN: The text suggests laughter serves as 'a signal of safety and playfulness.'|CN: 文章认为笑声是'安全和嬉戏的信号。'

questions.question: Primates and rodents exhibit vocalizations resembling laughter during play.
questions.explanation: EN: The text states 'Primates and even some rodents exhibit vocalizations that resemble laughter during rough-and-tumble play.'|CN: 文章指出'灵长类动物甚至一些啮齿动物在打闹玩耍时会发出类似笑声的声音。'

questions.question: Complete the sentence: The ability to detect genuine laughter versus fake laughter relies on different _______ pathways.
questions.explanation: EN: The text states detecting genuine vs fake laughter 'relies on different neural pathways.'|CN: 文章指出区分真笑和假笑'依赖于不同的神经通路。'

questions.question: What type of humor does the passage describe as nearly universal? (One word answer)
questions.explanation: EN: The text mentions humor 'involving incongruity or surprise - seem to be nearly universal.'|CN: 文章提到'涉及不一致或惊喜的幽默——似乎是近乎普遍的。'

passages.title: Global Food Security
passages.text:
Food security exists when all people, at all times, have physical, social, and economic access to sufficient, safe, and nutritious food that meets their dietary needs and food preferences. Despite significant progress in recent decades, the challenge of achieving global food security remains immense.

The Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) estimates that nearly 690 million people, or 8.9% of the world population, are undernourished. The majority of these individuals live in developing countries, particularly in sub-Saharan Africa and South Asia. Conflict is a primary driver of food insecurity, with 60% of the world's hungry living in conflict-affected areas.

Climate change presents a growing threat to food security. Rising temperatures, changing precipitation patterns, and increased frequency of extreme weather events are already affecting crop yields. Studies project that without adaptation, climate change could reduce global crop yields by up to 25% by 2050. This is particularly concerning given that the global population is expected to reach 9.7 billion by the same year.

Food waste is another critical issue. Approximately one-third of all food produced globally - about 1.3 billion tonnes per year - is lost or wasted. In developed countries, most waste occurs at the consumer level, while in developing countries, it occurs primarily during production, harvesting, and storage due to inadequate infrastructure.

Technological innovations offer potential solutions. Precision agriculture uses data from satellites and sensors to optimize irrigation, fertilization, and pest control, reducing resource use while increasing yields. Vertical farming allows crops to be grown in controlled environments in urban areas, reducing transportation costs and water usage. Lab-grown meat and plant-based proteins are emerging as alternatives to traditional animal agriculture, which has a significant environmental footprint.

Achieving global food security will require coordinated action across multiple fronts: reducing conflict, adapting to climate change, minimizing food waste, promoting sustainable agricultural practices, and ensuring that economic growth benefits the most vulnerable populations.

questions.question: What is the best definition of food security according to the passage?
options: A. Having enough food for one month
B. Access to sufficient, safe, and nutritious food at all times
C. Being able to grow your own food
D. Having access to imported food
questions.correctAnswer: B. Access to sufficient, safe, and nutritious food at all times
questions.explanation: EN: The passage defines food security as 'when all people, at all times, have physical, social, and economic access to sufficient, safe, and nutritious food.'|CN: 文章将粮食安全定义为'任何时候，所有人都能通过物质、社会和经济途径获得充足、安全和营养的食物。'

questions.question: The FAO estimates that less than 8% of the world's population is undernourished.
questions.explanation: EN: The text says '8.9% of the world population' are undernourished, which is more than 8%.|CN: 文章说世界人口的'8.9%'处于营养不良状态，高于8%。

questions.question: 60% of the world's hungry people live in countries affected by conflict.
questions.explanation: EN: The text states '60% of the world's hungry living in conflict-affected areas.'|CN: 文章指出'全球60%的饥饿人口生活在受冲突影响的地区。'

questions.question: According to the passage, what percentage could crop yields be reduced by climate change by 2050?
questions.explanation: EN: The text states 'climate change could reduce global crop yields by up to 25% by 2050.'|CN: 文章指出'气候变化可能使全球作物产量到2050年减少高达25%。'

questions.question: Complete the sentence: Approximately _______ of all food produced globally is lost or wasted each year.
questions.explanation: EN: The text states 'Approximately one-third of all food produced globally... is lost or wasted.'|CN: 文章指出'全球生产的所有食物中约三分之一被损失或浪费。'

questions.question: In developed countries, most food waste occurs during production and harvesting.
questions.explanation: EN: The text says in developed countries 'most waste occurs at the consumer level.'|CN: 文章指出在发达国家'大多数浪费发生在消费者层面。'

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT mentioned as a technological innovation for food security?
questions.explanation: EN: The text mentions precision agriculture, vertical farming, and lab-grown meat, but not GMOs.|CN: 文章提到了精准农业、垂直农业和实验室培养肉，但没有提到转基因生物。

questions.question: Complete the sentence: The global population is expected to reach _______ billion by 2050.
questions.explanation: EN: The text states 'the global population is expected to reach 9.7 billion by the same year (2050).'|CN: 文章指出'全球人口预计到2050年将达到97亿。'

questions.question: What is the primary driver of food insecurity mentioned in the passage? (One word answer)
questions.explanation: EN: The text states 'Conflict is a primary driver of food insecurity.'|CN: 文章指出'冲突是粮食不安全的主要驱动因素。'

questions.question: Most undernourished people live in sub-Saharan Africa and South Asia.
questions.explanation: EN: The text states 'The majority of these individuals live in developing countries, particularly in sub-Saharan Africa and South Asia.'|CN: 文章指出'这些人大多数生活在发展中国家，特别是撒哈拉以南非洲和南亚。'

questions.question: Complete the sentence: Precision agriculture uses data from satellites and _______ to optimize farming.
questions.explanation: EN: The text states 'Precision agriculture uses data from satellites and sensors.'|CN: 文章指出'精准农业利用卫星和传感器的数据。'

questions.question: What amount of food is wasted globally each year? (Include number and unit)
questions.explanation: EN: The text states 'about 1.3 billion tonnes per year - is lost or wasted.'|CN: 文章指出'每年约13亿吨被损失或浪费。'

questions.question: What does the passage say is needed to achieve global food security?
options: C. Coordinated action across multiple fronts
questions.correctAnswer: C. Coordinated action across multiple fronts
questions.explanation: EN: The passage concludes that achieving food security 'will require coordinated action across multiple fronts.'|CN: 文章总结道实现粮食安全'需要在多个方面采取协调行动。'

passages.title: The Psychology of Color
passages.text:
Color is a powerful communication tool that can influence mood, behavior, and even physiological responses. The field of color psychology examines how different colors affect human perception and action.

Red is one of the most potent colors in the spectrum. It naturally draws attention and is associated with both danger and excitement. Studies have shown that exposure to red can increase heart rate and create a sense of urgency. This is why red is commonly used for stop signs, sale tags, and warning labels. In competitive settings, athletes wearing red have been found to have a statistically higher win rate.

Blue, in contrast, has a calming effect. It is associated with trust, stability, and intelligence. Many financial institutions and technology companies use blue in their branding to project reliability. Research suggests that blue can enhance creative thinking and promote productivity. However, too much blue can be perceived as cold or uninviting.

Yellow is the most visible color to the human eye, which is why it is used for taxis and warning signs. It is associated with optimism and warmth, but it can also cause visual fatigue when used excessively. In small amounts, yellow can stimulate mental activity and generate feelings of happiness.

Green evokes nature and tranquility. It is considered the most restful color for the eyes and is often used in hospitals and schools to create a calming environment. Green is also associated with growth, health, and environmental consciousness, making it popular for organic and sustainable brands.

Cultural context plays a crucial role in color interpretation. While white is associated with purity and weddings in Western cultures, it represents mourning in many Eastern cultures. Black, representing sophistication in fashion, signifies death in many traditions. Purple, historically associated with royalty due to the rarity of purple dye, continues to convey luxury and creativity.

Marketers and designers apply color psychology extensively. The choice of color can affect how a product is perceived and can influence purchasing decisions. Studies suggest that up to 90% of snap judgments about products can be based on color alone. Understanding these associations is essential for effective communication in visual design.

questions.question: What is the main topic of this passage?
options: A. The science of how the eye perceives color
B. How colors affect human perception and behavior
C. The history of color in art
D. Color preferences across different age groups
questions.correctAnswer: B. How colors affect human perception and behavior
questions.explanation: EN: The passage discusses color psychology and how different colors influence mood, behavior, and perception.|CN: 文章讨论了色彩心理学以及不同颜色如何影响情绪、行为和感知。

questions.question: Red is associated with both danger and excitement.
questions.explanation: EN: The text states red 'is associated with both danger and excitement.'|CN: 文章指出红色'既与危险又与兴奋相关联。'

questions.question: Athletes wearing blue have a statistically higher win rate than those wearing red.
questions.explanation: EN: The text says 'athletes wearing red have been found to have a statistically higher win rate' - not blue.|CN: 文章说'穿着红色的运动员在统计上有更高的胜率'——不是蓝色。

questions.question: Complete the sentence: Blue is associated with trust, stability, and _______.
questions.explanation: EN: The text states 'Blue... is associated with trust, stability, and intelligence.'|CN: 文章指出'蓝色...与信任、稳定和智慧相关联。'

questions.question: Why is yellow used for taxis and warning signs?
options: A. It is associated with optimism
B. It is the most visible color to the human eye
D. It creates feelings of happiness
questions.correctAnswer: B. It is the most visible color to the human eye
questions.explanation: EN: The text states 'Yellow is the most visible color to the human eye, which is why it is used for taxis and warning signs.'|CN: 文章指出'黄色是人眼最易看见的颜色，这就是为什么它被用于出租车和警告标志。'

questions.question: Green is considered the most restful color for the eyes.
questions.explanation: EN: The text states 'Green is considered the most restful color for the eyes.'|CN: 文章指出'绿色被认为是对眼睛最舒适的颜色。'

questions.question: In which field is color psychology extensively applied according to the passage? (Give the role, e.g. 'Scientists')
questions.explanation: EN: The text states 'Marketers and designers apply color psychology extensively.'|CN: 文章指出'营销人员和设计师广泛运用色彩心理学。'

questions.question: White represents purity in all cultures around the world.
questions.explanation: EN: The text states that white 'represents mourning in many Eastern cultures' - not purity in all cultures.|CN: 文章指出白色'在许多东方文化中代表哀悼'——并非在所有文化中都代表纯洁。

questions.question: Why was purple historically associated with royalty?
options: A. It was the favorite color of kings
B. Purple dye was very rare
C. It represented power in nature
D. Religious texts described it as royal
questions.correctAnswer: B. Purple dye was very rare
questions.explanation: EN: The text states 'historically associated with royalty due to the rarity of purple dye.'|CN: 文章指出'由于紫色染料的稀缺性，历史上与皇室相关联。'

questions.question: Complete the sentence: Up to _______ of snap judgments about products can be based on color alone.
questions.explanation: EN: The text states 'up to 90% of snap judgments about products can be based on color alone.'|CN: 文章指出'高达90%对产品的快速判断仅基于颜色。'

questions.question: What is the main idea of the paragraph about red (Paragraph 2)?
options: A. Red is the most popular color
B. Red has powerful effects on attention and physiology
C. Red should only be used for warnings
questions.correctAnswer: B. Red has powerful effects on attention and physiology
questions.explanation: EN: Paragraph 2 discusses how red draws attention, affects heart rate, and creates urgency.|CN: 第二段讨论了红色如何吸引注意力、影响心率并制造紧迫感。

questions.question: Complete the sentence: Too much blue can be perceived as _______ or uninviting.
questions.explanation: EN: The text states 'too much blue can be perceived as cold or uninviting.'|CN: 文章指出'过多的蓝色可能被认为是冷漠或不友好的。'

questions.question: What color is said to enhance creative thinking according to the passage? (One word answer)
questions.explanation: EN: The text states 'Research suggests that blue can enhance creative thinking and promote productivity.'|CN: 文章指出'研究表明蓝色可以增强创造性思维并提高生产力。'

questions.question: Black represents sophistication in fashion according to the passage.
questions.explanation: EN: The text states 'Black, representing sophistication in fashion.'|CN: 文章指出'黑色在时尚中代表精致。'
